text: WHO was established on 7 April 1948.
WHO headquarters are in Geneva, Switzerland.
Measles is a highly contagious disease caused by a virus.
Malaria is a life-threatening disease spread to humans by some mosquitoes.
Tuberculosis is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis bacteria.
HIV can be transmitted through blood, breast milk, semen, and vaginal fluids.
Diabetes is a chronic metabolic disease characterized by elevated blood glucose.
Cancer is a leading cause of death worldwide.
Hypertension is diagnosed when blood pressure is too high.
Tobacco kills millions of people each year.
Air pollution is a major environmental risk to health.
Physical inactivity is a risk factor for noncommunicable diseases.
Antimicrobial resistance occurs when microbes no longer respond to medicines.
Depression is a common mental disorder.
Dementia affects memory, thinking, and everyday activities.
Obesity is abnormal or excessive fat accumulation that presents a health risk.
Exclusive breastfeeding is recommended for the first six months of life.
Vaccination is a proven tool for controlling infectious diseases.
Cholera is an acute diarrhoeal infection.
Ebola virus disease is a severe and often fatal illness in humans.
Rabies is a vaccine-preventable viral disease.
Polio is a highly infectious viral disease.
Hepatitis B is a viral infection that attacks the liver.
Hepatitis C is a liver disease caused by the hepatitis C virus.
Dengue is a viral infection transmitted through infected mosquitoes.
Yellow fever is an acute viral haemorrhagic disease transmitted by infected mosquitoes.
Influenza is an acute respiratory infection caused by influenza viruses.
COVID-19 is caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.
Safe drinking-water is essential to health.
Lead exposure can affect multiple body systems.
